name: Thai Country-Style Steamed Fish in Garlic Lime Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 pound whole fish, trout, sea bass, or perch
2 green onions, cut in half lengthwise
2 stalks lemon grass, using only the tender inner portion
recipeInstructions:
1. Scale and clean the fish, then rinse with cold water. Pat dry with paper towels.
2. Make diagonal slashes (about 2 inches apart) on both sides of the fish, stopping just short of the bone.
3. Place the fish on a heat-proof plate that is at least 1 inch smaller in diameter than your wok.
4. In a bowl, mix together diced green chiles, chopped garlic, fish sauce, lime juice, and chicken stock. Adjust the seasoning to achieve a predominantly sour taste.
5. Pour the mixture over the fish.
6. Scatter the sliced green onions and lemon grass pieces over the fish.
7. Fill the wok with about 2 inches of boiling water. Place the fish plate on a rack or trivet above the water.
8. Cover tightly and reduce heat to medium-high. Steam for 12 minutes, or until the meat is opaque white and cooked through.
9. Garnish the fish with cilantro sprigs and serve with the cooking juices. Accompany with steamed rice. Serves 4 with other entrees.
